skill.name: ai-voice-cloning
skill.description: AI voice generation, text-to-speech, and voice synthesis via inference.sh CLI. Models: Kokoro TTS, DIA, Chatterbox, Higgs, VibeVoice for natural speech. Capabilities: multiple voices, emotions, accents, long-form narration, conversation. Use for: voiceovers, audiobooks, podcasts, video narration, accessibility. Triggers: voice cloning, tts, text to speech, ai voice, voice generation, voice synthesis, voice over, narration, speech synthesis, ai narrator, elevenlabs alternative, natural voice, realistic speech, voice ai
skill.summary: Generate natural AI voices using multiple TTS models through the inference.sh CLI platform
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All 58 static findings are false positives from Markdown code blocks in documentation. External commands are shell examples demonstrating inference.sh CLI usage, not executable code. Network URLs are documentation links and example URLs. The skill is purely instructional documentation with no executable code or security risks.

medium_findings.title: Installation Script Pattern in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The documentation shows curl | sh installation pattern at SKILL.md:16. This is a legitimate installation method for the inference.sh CLI with SHA-256 verification noted at SKILL.md:25. The pattern is in documentation only, not executed by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Shell Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: Code examples contain shell command patterns using backticks in Markdown. These are documentation examples showing how to use the external inference.sh CLI tool, not executable code. All 48 external command detections are from Markdown code blocks.

low_findings.title: URL References in Documentation
low_findings.description: Network URLs found in documentation are links to inference.sh service and example URLs for video/image processing. These are documentation references, not actual network requests made by the skill.

content.user_title: Generate Natural AI Voiceovers for Any Project
content.value_statement: Create professional voiceovers and narration without recording equipment. This skill provides access to multiple AI text-to-speech models with natural voices in various styles and accents.
actual_capabilities: Generate natural speech from text using Kokoro TTS, DIA, Chatterbox, Higgs, and VibeVoice models
Select from multiple voice presets with different genders, accents, and speaking styles
Adjust speech speed from 0.8 to 1.2 for different content types
Process long-form content by splitting and merging audio chunks
Combine generated voiceovers with video content
limitations: Requires external inference.sh CLI installation and account
Content over 5000 characters must be split into chunks
Voice cloning capability requires the external service infrastructure
Audio quality depends on the selected model and input text formatting

use_cases.title: Create Video Voiceovers
use_cases.description: Generate professional narration for YouTube videos, tutorials, or marketing content using AI voices that match your brand tone

use_cases.title: Produce Audiobooks
use_cases.description: Convert written books or articles into audio format with consistent narration voices and appropriate pacing for long-form content

use_cases.title: Build Podcast Episodes
use_cases.description: Generate dialogue between multiple AI speakers to create podcast content or automate production of regular audio segments

prompt_templates.title: Basic Voice Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a voiceover reading: '{{text}}' using a {{style}} voice at normal speed
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate simple speech from text with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Professional Narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional narration of this business text: '{{text}}'. Use an authoritative male voice at 1.0 speed. Pause appropriately at commas and periods.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate corporate narration for presentations or earnings calls

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Voice Conversation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dialogue between two speakers. Speaker 1 (male, conversational): '{{speaker1_text}}'. Speaker 2 (female, friendly): '{{speaker2_text}}'. Merge both audio files with 300ms crossfade.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate conversation between two different AI voices

prompt_templates.title: Long-Form Audiobook
prompt_templates.prompt: Split this text into chunks under 5000 characters: '{{long_text}}'. Generate each chunk using voice 'bf_emma' at 0.9 speed. Merge all chunks with 100ms crossfade.
prompt_templates.scenario: Process long text content by chunking and merging audio

output_examples.input: Generate professional narration: 'Welcome to our annual review. Revenue increased 25% year-over-year.'
output_examples.output: Generated audio file containing professional male voice reading the text at normal speed with clear pronunciation and appropriate pauses

output_examples.input: Create conversational dialogue between Sarah and Michael discussing AI developments
output_examples.output: Two separate audio files merged into one conversation, with female voice (Sarah) and male voice (Michael) alternating lines with smooth crossfade transitions

output_examples.input: Generate slow-paced meditation narration: 'Take a deep breath. Let yourself relax. Feel the tension melt away.'
output_examples.output: Audio file with warm female voice reading at 0.8 speed, extended pauses after each sentence, and calming intonation

best_practices: Match voice style to content type - use professional voices for business, casual voices for social media
Use punctuation strategically to control pacing - periods create full pauses, commas create brief pauses
Keep sentences under 25 words for more natural-sounding speech
Test different voice presets with the same text to find the best match
Process long content in chunks under 5000 characters to maintain consistency
anti_patterns: Do not generate extremely long texts in one operation - split content over 5000 characters into chunks
Avoid using exclamation marks excessively as they can create unnatural emphasis
Do not forget to merge audio chunks when processing long-form content
Avoid using very fast speeds (above 1.2) as speech becomes difficult to understand

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI tool using the install script provided in the skill documentation. The installation requires a login to the inference.sh service.

faq.question: Can I clone any voice?
faq.answer: This skill provides pre-configured voice presets across different models. Voice cloning of specific individuals requires the external inference.sh service infrastructure and account access.

faq.question: What is the maximum text length?
faq.answer: Single requests work best under 5000 characters. For longer content, split the text into chunks, generate audio for each chunk separately, then merge using the media-merger tool.

faq.question: Which voice model should I use?
faq.answer: Kokoro TTS is best for natural multi-voice content, DIA for conversational speech, Higgs for professional narration, Chatterbox for casual content, and VibeVoice for emotional range.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: The skill is licensed under MIT, but the inference.sh service has its own terms. Check the inference.sh service agreement for commercial usage rights and pricing.

faq.question: How do I add background music?
faq.answer: Generate your voiceover first, then use the media-merger tool referenced in the skill documentation to combine the voice audio with background music or video files.
